assumptions: A declared intragroup dividend that has been recognised as a receivable/payable is treated as an intragroup monetary item.
The analysis is limited to foreign currency hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is the key exception in IFRS 9 for recognised intragroup balances in consolidation when the foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: General exclusion for intragroup items in consolidation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This defines the default rule that only items with external parties qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements unless a specific exception applies.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is the separate IFRS 9 exception relevant if the analysis is made before the dividend receivable is recognised and only where the foreign currency risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.
